what_it_does: Documents a patched Jinja chat template that inverts Qwen3.5's default thinking mode so reasoning is off by default, and explains how the patched file is loaded by a vLLM serve script.
observations: The default Qwen3.5 template opens a thinking block by default; the patch makes the default an empty think/think-close block.
The patched template is passed to vLLM via --chat-template in ~/vllm-tests/serve.sh.
The model's bundled chat_template.jinja in the Hugging Face cache is a symlink to a blob, so editing in place is avoided.
Callers can still opt into thinking with chat_template_kwargs: {'enable_thinking': true}.
Tool calling and TTFT latency are cited as reasons for disabling default thinking.
Family coverage is tracked: qwen3_5-4b-awq is patched and deployed; qwen3_5-0_8b is verified to have the same template structure; other variants are unverified.
Open questions include whether to centralize chat-template handling as more models join.
standouts: Inverts the enable_thinking condition rather than removing thinking support entirely.
Uses a local file override because the HF cache template is a symlinked blob.
Connects model deployment latency and tool-calling behavior to a template-level default.
conventions: Operations note format with front matter (type, title, date_started, tags).
Uses wiki-style internal links to related model and operations pages.
Includes before/after code blocks and a caller-behavior table.
Separates verified, unverified, and non-applicable model coverage.
concerns: The patched template path ~/vllm-tests/chat_template_nothink.jinja is outside the repository, so the doc may drift from the actual file.
The claim that enable_thinking: true produces useful reasoning is marked uncharacterized.
Open items are not assigned owners or dates.
notes: This operations note records why and how the Qwen3.5 chat template was patched to disable default thinking via a local vLLM --chat-template override.
questions: Where exactly is the canonical source of the patched template relative to this repo?
What is the current deployment status of qwen3_5-0_8b?
Has the patched default been validated for tool-calling correctness and TTFT on the deployed model?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the chat-template patch and its deployment mechanics, which matches the file's content.
apparent_themes: Default behavior should favor low-latency, tool-call-safe output over vendor reasoning defaults.
Prefer non-destructive overrides to cached model artifacts.
Per-model template verification is required even within a model family.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the deployed Qwen3.5 model behave like a direct-answer assistant by default while preserving an opt-in reasoning path, and to record the operational rationale so future model additions can follow the same pattern.
novelty: The inversion of a bundled reasoning-mode default through a local chat-template override, with explicit family-coverage tracking.
contribution: Captures the exact template inversion, the loading mechanism, the rationale around latency and tool calling, and a model-family coverage map that a fused system would need to reproduce or audit the deployment default.
summary_for_embedding: An operations note documenting a patched Qwen3.5 chat template that disables default thinking mode via a local vLLM --chat-template override, with caller behavior and family coverage details.
intention_for_embedding: To record why the Qwen3.5 default thinking behavior was inverted and how the patched template is deployed and opted into.
